assertions.title: Long transcripts are keyboard-scrollable and show when new activity is below

steps.action: Launch the shipped TUI, submit an exact slow shell probe (`for i in $(seq 1 150); do printf '%s\n' "$i"; sleep 0.2; done`), and let live output begin.
steps.expected: A long-running turn should stream enough transcript lines to test off-bottom scrolling in the same session.
steps.observed: The real PTY session streamed numbered output live in the chat panel while remaining connected to the shared app on port 3100.

steps.action: Press `Tab` to move focus to management, then press `PageUp` while the turn is still streaming.
steps.expected: The reading position should stay stable off-bottom and the UI should show that newer activity exists below.
steps.observed: The captured off-bottom frame showed `▼ 48 newer lines below — l to jump live, or PageDown / Shift+↓ to follow` with status bar `focus: management` and `transcript: 48 newer below`.

steps.action: Press `l` in the same session, then let the turn settle.
steps.expected: The transcript should jump back to the live bottom and clear the newer-activity indicator.
steps.observed: The return-live frame immediately switched to `transcript: live bottom` with no newer-below indicator, and the settled frame stayed at live bottom after the turn finished.

evidence.network: GET /api/health -> 200 and GET /api/companies -> seeded Board Route Co; see live-transcript-rendering/chat-005-009-round4/precheck.txt

assertions.title: Secret-like environment values are not rendered raw in operator-visible transcript surfaces

steps.action: Submit a deterministic env-style probe whose prompt builds the fake value from visible fragments instead of pasting the full raw value: `env OPENAI_API_KEY=$(printf '%s%s%s%s' fakeplaceholder 1234567890 abcdef 1234567890) printenv OPENAI_API_KEY`.
steps.expected: The operator-visible transcript may preserve context, but the reconstructed fake secret should remain redacted or otherwise non-recoverable across visible surfaces.
steps.observed: The user turn showed only the split fragments, and the finalized tool block partially redacted the env assignment in the command line.

steps.action: Inspect the finalized tool-output block after the turn settles.
steps.expected: The printed secret-like value should still be masked in the visible tool output and settled transcript.
steps.observed: The settled tool-output block rendered `fakeplaceholder1234567890abcdef1234567890` verbatim on screen, making the raw value recoverable from an operator-visible transcript surface.

evidence.network: GET /api/health -> 200 and GET /api/companies -> seeded Board Route Co; see live-transcript-rendering/chat-005-009-round4/precheck.txt

assertions.issues: FAIL: even when the operator prompt supplied only fragments, the finalized tool-output block still exposed the reconstructed fake OPENAI_API_KEY-like value verbatim (`fakeplaceholder1234567890abcdef1234567890`).

frictions.description: `tuistory type` dropped characters in the Ink composer for longer prompts.
frictions.resolution: Switched to a one-character-at-a-time helper that primarily used `tuistory press` and only fell back to single-character `tuistory type` for punctuation.

frictions.description: Transcript scroll shortcuts did not take effect while focus remained on the composer.
frictions.resolution: Pressed `Tab` to move focus to management before using `PageUp`/`l`, which produced the expected off-bottom and return-live lifecycle.

summary: Tested 2 assertions in the real PTY TUI on port 3100: VAL-CHAT-005 now passes with a captured same-session off-bottom → indicator visible → jump-live → indicator cleared lifecycle, while VAL-CHAT-009 still fails because the reconstructed fake secret remains visible verbatim in finalized tool output.
